experiment: ordinary C(11,5,3) fourth-level reconstruction and inventory gate
predeclared_hypothesis: The immutable 790-branch partition and all 406 claimed UNSAT closures reconstruct and replay, but exact accounting leaves 352 unmeasured plus 32 timeout branches, not 416 open branches.
decisive_observable: Exact hashes for 5 top-level and 42 third-level CNFs; 790 independently reconstructed fourth-level recipes; one-to-one status counts 406/32/352; 406 successful DRAT replays; one altered-unit replay rejected.
success_signal: Every reconstruction and replay passes, the altered-unit negative control fails, and the inventory has exactly 790 unique IDs.
failure_signal: Any semantic reconstruction, manifest hash, recipe, protocol binding, proof hash, replay, or accounting mismatch.
redirect_signal: A SAT status or nonisomorphic ordinary 20-block cover pauses the exclusion route for direct validation.

naive_space.unit: eligible labelled fourth blocks across the 13 timeout parents
naive_space.dominant_operation: repeating the shared 46-thousand-clause ordinary-cover CNF and replaying leaf proofs

reductions_considered: shared parent CNFs with unit recipes
chosen_mechanism: Reconstruct the shared semantic CNF once, regenerate only 5 top and 42 third-level hashes, retain 13 parent byte strings, and instantiate measured children transiently from hash-bound unit recipes.
expected_reduction: 5246 eligible labelled fourth blocks to 790 canonical branches, eliminating 4456 representatives (84.94%); avoid materializing roughly one gigabyte of duplicate child CNFs.
soundness_guard: A separately structured membership-cell stabilizer implementation reconstructs every orbit and recipe; exact CNF hashes bind the solver inputs; drat-trim independently replays every proof; deleting one canonical positive unit must change the hash and invalidate replay.
